content: Start with one short warm background question about what kind of design or research work she is closest to lately. Then ask one short follow-up about how involved she usually is in feature-priority or roadmap conversations versus synthesis and prototype work. Use these as opening context before any recent decision probe.
superseded_question: 講下你最近一次要定 roadmap 或功能優先次序嗰陣，實際上發生咗咩事？

content: Do not introduce the AI concept yet. Stay on one recent real decision where prototype feedback, interview notes, or user reactions affected feature priority or product direction. Ask specifically what she was trying to understand, what evidence conflicted or felt shallow, what nuance risk she saw, what she could defend in the room versus what still felt uncertain to her, and what tradeoff was actually made.
superseded_question: 嗰次你實際係點樣由啲訪談內容搵到嗰啲猶豫位同重複問題？

content: Once the current-state decision is concrete enough, then introduce the concept neutrally. After that, ask what existing synthesis or preparation step it would replace or shorten, what would make it flatten nuance or edge cases, and what she would do if synthetic output looks neat but contradicts messier real user material.
superseded_question: 有冇一次都係做前期 review，但你就算早知有人會喺決定位猶豫，都唔會加多一層呢類工具？
